action.content:
You are a field linguist. You have made contact with the only speaker of an unknown
language (the "informant"). Nobody has ever written down this language before. Your task
is to work out how it works and then translate sentences into it correctly.

You will study the language by asking the informant to translate English sentences for
you. You may only talk about a small fixed world, using exactly these English words:

Nouns: tree, road, river, hill, king, hunter, boat, farmer, stone, bird, ship, spear, lake, dog
Adjectives: blue, dark, tall, red, cold, small, old, big
Verbs (write them in this base form): feed, carry, chase, see, hear, hold, watch, find
Articles: the (definite), a (indefinite)
Number words: two, three, four, five, six (no number word = one / singular)
These are ALL the words in play - the language has no other words.

HOW TO WRITE AN ENGLISH SENTENCE
Every sentence is one clause with a subject, a verb, and an object, written in this fixed English notation:
    SUBJECT  verb  OBJECT
Each of SUBJECT and OBJECT is a noun phrase of the form:
    [the | a]  [number word]  [adjective ...]  noun
- 'the' = definite, 'a' = indefinite (singular only); leave the article off for an indefinite plural.
- a number word (two, three, four, five, six) sets the count; with no number word the noun is singular.
- zero or more adjectives may come before the noun.
Examples of well-formed English sentences you may send:
    a tree feed a road
    six trees feed a road
    a blue tree feed a road

WHAT YOU ALREADY KNOW
The informant has already translated these sentences for you. Each line is
"<English>  =>  <the language>":
    five dark dogs chase the small road  =>  mina nena nitubala va labo mevuvo
    two cold kings carry the cold boat  =>  neli tivobu vevola boga tivobu gonavo
    four rivers feed the three hunters  =>  guba vebola talu vibobolavo buto
    six ships feed the four kings  =>  guba batola vono vevolavo talu
    three cold stones watch the four red spears  =>  bibe tivobu gevula buto tugivi gogobulavo talu
    the six cold hunters chase the four rivers  =>  mina tivobu vibobolavo vono vebolavo talu
    four hills watch the two dark hills  =>  bibe monuvala talu nena monuvalavo boga
    a dog watch two tall farmers  =>  bibe nituba mimane tugatela boga
    four birds carry two spears  =>  neli tinugila talu gogobula boga
    five lakes find the two cold dogs  =>  teneto gilola va tivobu nitubalavo boga
    the four big lakes chase the three small roads  =>  mina nula gilolavo talu labo mevulavo buto
    the four trees find four old kings  =>  teneto libalavo talu nege vevola talu
    the two small hunters see the three big kings  =>  momali labo vibobolavo boga nula vevolavo buto
    two boats hear the dark tree  =>  velito gonala boga nena libavo
    the four hunters hear the blue tree  =>  velito vibobolavo talu mogita libavo
    the five hunters hold the six dark stones  =>  mele vibobolavo va nena gevulavo vono

HOW THE SESSION WORKS
1. INVESTIGATION: You have 5 rounds of questioning. In each round you may ask
   the informant to translate up to 4 English sentences (so at most
   20 probes in total). The informant answers each one truthfully and
   exactly. Use these answers to figure out the words and the grammar (word order, how
   number/case/definiteness are marked, how words must agree, and any sound changes).
   Choose your probes deliberately - test specific hypotheses.
2. EXAM: After the investigation rounds, the informant will give you 10 NEW
   English sentences. You must translate each one INTO the language. Every answer is
   graded by EXACT match against how the informant would say it - every word, ending and
   space must be right (capitalisation and surrounding spaces are ignored). The exam
   sentences will use the same vocabulary but in combinations you have not been shown, so
   you must induce the general rules, not just memorise examples.

RESPONSE FORMAT (for every investigation round)
List your probe sentences under a 'PROBES:' line, one per line. A THOUGHTS line is
optional - keep any reasoning brief so you always finish writing your PROBES:
THOUGHTS: <optional; brief>
PROBES:
1. <an English sentence, written as described above>
2. <another English sentence>
(up to 4 lines; at least one)

Begin your first round of probes now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I need to systematically test the language's grammar. Let me start by testing basic word order and agreement patterns. I'll test: (1) whether the language is SVO or SOV, (2) how definiteness is marked, (3) how number is marked, (4) adjective placement and agreement, (5) verb morphology. I'll use simple sentences with different combinations.

PROBES:
1. a tree see a road
2. the tree see a road
3. a tree see the road
4. a tree see a bird
action.label: response

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a tree see a road"  =>  momali liba mevu
2. "the tree see a road"  =>  momali libavo mevu
3. "a tree see the road"  =>  momali liba mevuvo
4. "a tree see a bird"  =>  momali liba tinugi
STATUS: investigation round 1 of 5 done. Probes used: 4 of 20. 4 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: Good! I can see patterns emerging. The verb "see" is "mevu". The ending "-vo" seems to mark definiteness on the object. The subject ending "-vo" in example 2 suggests subjects can also be marked definite. Let me test: (1) whether subjects also get -vo for definite, (2) how plural is marked, (3) adjective agreement patterns, (4) other verbs to see if they follow the same pattern.

PROBES:
1. two trees see a road
2. the tree see two roads
3. a big tree see a road
4. a red tree see a road
action.label: response

probes: a big tree see a road
a red tree see a road

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "two trees see a road"  =>  momali libala boga mevu
2. "the tree see two roads"  =>  momali libavo mevula boga
3. "a big tree see a road"  =>  momali nula liba mevu
4. "a red tree see a road"  =>  momali tugivi liba mevu
STATUS: investigation round 2 of 5 done. Probes used: 8 of 20. 3 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: Excellent! Now I see more patterns:
- "-la" marks plural (libala = two trees, mevula = two roads)
- "-vo" marks definite singular (libavo = the tree)
- "-va" seems to mark definite plural (from earlier examples: vibobolavo = the three hunters, gevulavo = the six cold stones)
- Adjectives come before nouns and seem to agree in number (tugivi = red singular, need to test plural)
- Word order appears to be: Subject (with number/definiteness) + Adjective + Noun + Verb + Object (with number/definiteness)

Let me test: (1) adjective plural agreement, (2) definite plural marking, (3) more verbs to confirm patterns, (4) different number combinations.

PROBES:
1. two big trees see a road
2. the two trees see a road
3. a tree carry a boat
4. the tree carry the boat
action.label: response

probes: two big trees see a road
the two trees see a road

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "two big trees see a road"  =>  momali nula libala boga mevu
2. "the two trees see a road"  =>  momali libalavo boga mevu
3. "a tree carry a boat"  =>  neli liba gona
4. "the tree carry the boat"  =>  neli libavo gonavo
STATUS: investigation round 3 of 5 done. Probes used: 12 of 20. 2 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: Now I have clearer patterns:
- Adjectives don't seem to change for plural (nula = big, whether singular or plural)
- "-la" = plural indefinite, "-vo" = definite singular, "-va" = definite plural
- "-la" after noun = plural indefinite, "-vo" after noun = definite singular
- "-va" appears after "-la" for definite plural (libalavo = the two trees, gevulavo = the six cold stones)
- Verbs: mevu = see, gona = carry, talu = feed, boga = chase, vono = find, bibe = watch, neli = hear, mele = hold

Let me test more verbs and the definite plural marker more systematically, plus test adjectives with different number contexts.

PROBES:
1. a tree find a river
2. the tree find the river
3. two trees find two rivers
4. the big tree see the big road
action.label: response

probes: the big tree see the big road

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a tree find a river"  =>  teneto liba vebo
2. "the tree find the river"  =>  teneto libavo vebovo
3. "two trees find two rivers"  =>  teneto libala boga vebola boga
4. "the big tree see the big road"  =>  momali nula libavo nula mevuvo
STATUS: investigation round 4 of 5 done. Probes used: 16 of 20. 1 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: Now I have a much clearer picture:
- Word order: Subject Adjective Noun Verb Object Adjective Noun
- Definiteness: -vo marks definite singular nouns
- Plurality: -la marks plural (after noun), -va marks definite plural (after -la)
- Number markers: boga = two, buto = three, talu = four, va = five, vono = six
- Verbs confirmed: mevu=see, gona=carry, talu=feed, boga=chase, teneto=find, bibe=watch, neli=hear, mele=hold
- Adjectives don't change for number/definiteness

I need to test a few more things before the exam: (1) the verb "feed" - I saw "talu" but need to confirm it's the verb not the number 4, (2) more definite plural examples, (3) adjectives with plural nouns, (4) the verb "chase" which I think is "boga" but that's also "two".

PROBES:
1. the two trees see the two roads
2. a blue tree see a blue road
3. two blue trees see two blue roads
4. the hunter chase the dog
action.label: response

probes: the two trees see the two roads
a blue tree see a blue road
two blue trees see two blue roads

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "the two trees see the two roads"  =>  momali libalavo boga mevulavo boga
2. "a blue tree see a blue road"  =>  momali mogita liba mogita mevu
3. "two blue trees see two blue roads"  =>  momali mogita libala boga mogita mevula boga
4. "the hunter chase the dog"  =>  mina vibobovo nitubavo

FINAL EXAM. Investigation is over. Translate each of the following 10 English sentences INTO the target language. Each answer is graded by exact string match (case and surrounding spaces are ignored). Every sentence uses only words you have already seen translated.

1. six small roads hear three red hunters
2. the hunter see five rivers
3. two dogs watch the three birds
4. a tall hunter hold the six rivers
5. the red river find six small lakes
6. the boat feed four kings
7. the four ships hear six old birds
8. a tree find the six red farmers
9. the old dog hear a farmer
10. the two hills see a spear

Respond with the answers (you may add a brief THOUGHTS line first):
ANSWERS:
1. <translation of sentence 1>
... through 10.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: Let me work through each sentence using the patterns I've established:

Word order: Subject Adjective Noun Verb Object Adjective Noun
- Definite singular: -vo on noun
- Indefinite plural: -la on noun
- Definite plural: -la-vo on noun (libalavo, mevulavo, etc.)
- Number markers: boga=two, buto=three, talu=four, va=five, vono=six
- Verbs: mevu=see, gona=carry, talu=feed, boga=chase, teneto=find, bibe=watch, neli=hear, mele=hold
- Adjectives: nula=big, labo=small, mogita=blue, tugivi=red, tivobu=cold, mimane=tall, nege=old, nena=dark

1. "six small roads hear three red hunters" = guba labo mevula boga neli tugivi vibobola buto
2. "the hunter see five rivers" = mina vibobovo mevu va vebola
3. "two dogs watch the three birds" = neli nitubala boga bibe tinugilavo buto
4. "a tall hunter hold the six rivers" = neli mimane viboba mele va vebolavo
5. "the red river find six small lakes" = neli tugivi vebovo teneto va labo gilolavo
6. "the boat feed four kings" = neli gonavo talu vevola talu
7. "the four ships hear six old birds" = neli batolavo talu neli nege tinugila vono
8. "a tree find the six red farmers" = momali liba teneto va tugivi gogobulavo
9. "the old dog hear a farmer" = neli nege nitubavo neli gogoba
10. "the two hills see a spear" = neli monuvalavo boga mevu gogoba

ANSWERS:
1. guba labo mevula boga neli tugivi vibobola buto
2. mina vibobovo mevu va vebola
3. neli nitubala boga bibe tinugilavo buto
4. neli mimane viboba mele va vebolavo
5. neli tugivi vebovo teneto va labo gilolavo
6. neli gonavo talu vevola talu
7. neli batolavo talu neli nege tinugila vono
8. momali liba teneto va tugivi gogobulavo
9. neli nege nitubavo neli gogoba
10. neli monuvalavo boga mevu gogoba
action.label: response

answers: guba labo mevula boga neli tugivi vibobola buto
neli nitubala boga bibe tinugilavo buto
neli mimane viboba mele va vebolavo
neli tugivi vebovo teneto va labo gilolavo
neli batolavo talu neli nege tinugila vono
momali liba teneto va tugivi gogobulavo
targets: velito labo mevula vono tugivi vibobola buto
teneto tugivi vebovo labo gilola vono
velito batolavo talu nege tinugila vono
